A.description: The letter A is formed by making a fist with your thumb resting on the side of your index finger.
tips: Make sure to keep your fingers curled tightly to form a clear fist.
Keep your thumb relaxed and resting on the side of your index finger.
Practice transitioning from other letters to A to improve your fluidity.

B.description: Flat hand with fingers together, thumb across palm
tips: Watch the hand animation carefully and follow the movement
Practice in front of a mirror to check your form
Keep your movements smooth and natural.
Watch the hand animation carefully and follow the movement
Practice in front of a mirror to check your form
Keep your movements smooth and natural.

D.description: Index finger up, other fingers touch thumb
tips: Watch the hand animation carefully and follow the movement
Practice in front of a mirror to check your form
Keep your movements smooth and natural.
Watch the hand animation carefully and follow the movement
Practice in front of a mirror to check your form
Keep your movements smooth and natural.

F.description: Index and thumb form circle, other fingers up
tips: Watch the hand animation carefully and follow the movement
Practice in front of a mirror to check your form
Keep your movements smooth and natural.
Watch the hand animation carefully and follow the movement
Practice in front of a mirror to check your form
Keep your movements smooth and natural.

H.description: Index and middle finger point horizontally
tips: Watch the hand animation carefully and follow the movement
Practice in front of a mirror to check your form
Keep your movements smooth and natural.

I.description: Pinky finger up, others closed with thumb
tips: Watch the hand animation carefully and follow the movement
Practice in front of a mirror to check your form
Keep your movements smooth and natural.

J.description: Pinky up, draw a J in the air
tips: Watch the hand animation carefully and follow the movement
Practice in front of a mirror to check your form
Keep your movements smooth and natural.

K.description: Index and middle up in V, thumb between them
tips: Watch the hand animation carefully and follow the movement
Practice in front of a mirror to check your form
Keep your movements smooth and natural.

L.description: Index and thumb form L shape.
tips: Watch the hand animation carefully and follow the movement
Practice in front of a mirror to check your form
Keep your movements smooth and natural.

M.description: Three fingers over thumb, pinky down
tips: Watch the hand animation carefully and follow the movement
Practice in front of a mirror to check your form
Keep your movements smooth and natural.

N.description: Two fingers over thumb, others down
tips: Watch the hand animation carefully and follow the movement
Practice in front of a mirror to check your form
Keep your movements smooth and natural.
Watch the hand animation carefully and follow the movement
Practice in front of a mirror to check your form
Keep your movements smooth and natural.

Keep your movements smooth and natural.
Watch the hand animation carefully and follow the movement
Practice in front of a mirror to check your form
Keep your movements smooth and natural.

V.description: Index and middle up in V shape
tips: Watch the hand animation carefully and follow the movement
Practice in front of a mirror to check your form
Keep your movements smooth and natural.

W.description: Three fingers up in W shape
tips: Watch the hand animation carefully and follow the movement
Practice in front of a mirror to check your form
Keep your movements smooth and natural.
Watch the hand animation carefully and follow the movement
Practice in front of a mirror to check your form
Keep your movements smooth and natural.
Watch the hand animation carefully and follow the movement
Practice in front of a mirror to check your form
Keep your movements smooth and natural.

Z.description: Index draws a Z in the air
tips: Watch the hand animation carefully and follow the movement
Practice in front of a mirror to check your form
Keep your movements smooth and natural.

Red.description: Use your index finger and brush it downward along your lips.
tips: Keep your index finger straight and relaxed.
Start near the upper lip and move slightly downward.
Practice the motion slowly to make the sign clear.

Blue.description: Make the letter B handshape and shake your hand slightly.
tips: Keep your fingers together and thumb across your palm.
Move the hand gently from side to side.
Keep the movement small and controlled.

Green.description: Form the letter G with your hand and shake it slightly.
tips: Extend your index finger and thumb parallel.
Keep the other fingers closed.
Move the hand slightly to emphasize the sign.

Yellow.description: Make the letter Y handshape and shake it gently.
tips: Extend your thumb and pinky.
Keep the other fingers folded.
Move the hand side to side slightly.

Black.description: Draw your index finger across your forehead.
tips: Start from one side of your forehead.
Move the finger across smoothly.
Keep the motion slow and clear.

White.description: Place your hand on your chest and pull it outward.
tips: Start with fingers touching the chest.
Move the hand forward slowly.
Keep fingers slightly open.

Pink.description: Form the letter P and tap it on your chin.
tips: Keep your fingers clearly in the P shape.
Touch the chin lightly.
Avoid large movements.

Purple.description: Make the letter P handshape and shake it slightly.
tips: Keep the P handshape steady.
Shake gently from the wrist.
Make sure the fingers remain clearly shaped.

Orange.description: Form a claw hand near your mouth and squeeze your fingers together.
tips: Start with fingers slightly curved.
Close the fingers toward the thumb repeatedly.
Keep the hand close to the mouth.

Gray.description: Both hands start near the chest with fingers relaxed, then move outward while opening slightly.
tips: Start with the hands relaxed near the upper chest area.
Keep the fingers naturally together at the beginning of the sign.
Move the hands outward smoothly while slightly opening the fingers.
Avoid stiff finger movements and keep the motion soft and fluid.
Watch the thumb position carefully and keep it relaxed against the hand.

0.description: Form an 'O' handshape by touching your fingertips together.
tips: Keep all fingertips touching.
Hand should be relaxed.
Palm can face forward.

1.description: Extend your index finger upward while keeping other fingers closed.
tips: Keep the other fingers tightly closed.
Palm faces forward.
Finger should be straight.

2.description: Extend index and middle fingers upward.
tips: Keep fingers separated slightly.
Palm faces forward.
Other fingers remain closed.

3.description: Extend thumb, index, and middle fingers.
tips: Ring and pinky remain closed.
Palm faces forward.
Fingers should be clearly visible.

4.description: Extend four fingers upward with thumb tucked in.
tips: Keep thumb inside the palm.
Fingers spread slightly.
Palm faces forward.
Spread fingers naturally.
Palm faces forward.
Keep hand steady.

6.description: Touch thumb to pinky finger while other fingers stay extended.
tips: Index, middle, and ring stay up.
Thumb touches pinky clearly.
Palm faces forward.

7.description: Touch thumb to ring finger while other fingers stay extended.
tips: Index and middle stay up.
Thumb touches ring finger clearly.
Palm faces forward.

8.description: Touch thumb to middle finger while other fingers stay extended.
tips: Index, ring, and pinky remain extended.
Thumb touches middle finger clearly.
Palm faces forward.

9.description: Touch thumb to index finger while other fingers stay extended.
tips: Ring, middle, and pinky stay extended.
Make a clear 'O' shape with thumb and index.
Palm faces forward.

10.description: Make a fist with thumb extended upward and shake slightly.
tips: Keep fingers closed tightly.
Thumb points upward.
Small wrist shake.

Hello.description: Bring your flat right hand up to your forehead or temple with fingers together, then sweep it outward and away in a salute-like motion.
tips: Start with the fingertips near the forehead.
The sweep outward should be smooth and confident.
Emphasize the wrist flicking outward at the end.
Keep fingers flat and together throughout.

Goodbye.description: Raise your right hand to about head height with fingers pointing up, then fold the fingers down and back up repeatedly in a waving motion.
tips: The wave comes from the fingers folding at the knuckles.
Keep the palm facing forward and hand raised.
Emphasize the wrist position staying firm.
Two to three finger folds is enough.

Please.description: Place your flat right hand on your chest with fingers together, then rub it in a circular motion on your chest.
tips: The hand stays in full contact with the chest.
The circle is made by rotating from the wrist.
Emphasize the wrist-driven circular motion.
Keep the hand flat and fingers together.

Sorry.description: Make a fist with your right hand with thumb extended and place it on your chest, then rub it in a circular motion.
tips: Unlike Please which uses a flat hand, Sorry uses a closed fist.
Emphasize the wrist rotation.
Keep the fist firm and in contact with the chest.

No.description: Extend your right index and middle fingers with the thumb tucked under, then snap them down to meet the thumb in a quick pinching motion.
tips: Start with the index and middle fingers clearly extended upward.
The snap down to the thumb should be quick and sharp.
Emphasize the downward wrist snap.
The motion can be repeated twice for emphasis.

Yes.description: Make a fist with your right hand and nod it up and down from the wrist, mimicking a head nodding yes.
tips: The motion is entirely from the wrist.
Keep a firm fist throughout.
Emphasize a clear and deliberate wrist nod.
Two nods is the standard.

Thank You.description: Touch the fingertips of your flat hand to your chin, then swing the hand forward and outward away from your face, ending with the palm facing up.
tips: The motion starts at the chin and flows outward.
Emphasize the wrist rotation as the hand swings away.
Keep fingers together and hand flat.
End with the palm clearly facing upward.

I Love You.description: Hold up your right hand with the thumb, index finger, and pinky extended, and middle and ring fingers folded down. Give the hand a small twist from right to left.
tips: Start the hand near the chin or lips.
Keep the fingers together and the movement smooth.
Move the hand from right to left while focused on the wrist's motion.

Deaf.description: Point your right index finger to your ear, then move it down and forward to touch the corner of your mouth.
tips: The movement is a clear straight path between the ear and the corner of the mouth.
Emphasize the wrist orientation shift.
Either direction is acceptable.
Keep the index finger fully extended throughout.

Hearing Person.description: Extend your right index finger and hold it in front of your mouth, then make a small forward circular loop motion with it.
tips: The circle is made in front of the mouth.
The looping motion comes from the wrist rotating.
Keep the index finger fully extended and pointing forward.
The circle is small and tight.

Me/I.description: Point to yourself using the index finger near the chest.
tips: Touch or point toward the center of your chest.
Keep the movement small and natural.
Use a relaxed hand and avoid stiff motions.
Maintain clear direction toward yourself.

My/Mine.description: Place an open hand flat against the chest.
tips: Keep the fingers together and palm open.
Touch the chest lightly with the palm.
Avoid bending the fingers too much.
Keep the motion calm and smooth.

You.description: Point outward toward the other person using the index finger.
tips: Extend the index finger clearly forward.
Aim naturally toward the person you mean.
Keep the wrist relaxed.
Avoid exaggerated arm movement.

Your/Yours.description: Move an open palm outward toward the other person.
tips: Keep the palm open with fingers together.
Face the palm toward the person.
Push the hand outward gently.
Keep the movement smooth and controlled.

He/She/It.description: Point toward the person or object being referenced.
tips: Use a clear pointing direction.
Keep the index finger straight.
Use natural facial focus toward the target.
Avoid fast or unclear movements.

His/Her/Its.description: Use an open palm directed toward the person or object.
tips: Keep the hand open and relaxed.
Move slightly toward the referenced subject.
Keep fingers together for clarity.
Maintain a gentle outward motion.

We.description: Move the index finger in a small arc between yourself and others.
tips: Start by pointing to yourself first.
Sweep the motion smoothly toward the group.
Keep the movement small and controlled.
Use relaxed wrist movement.

Our/Ours.description: Use an open palm moving in a small arc across the group area.
tips: Keep the palm open and fingers together.
Move the hand smoothly across the space.
Show inclusion with a gentle sweeping motion.
Avoid sharp or rushed movements.

They.description: Point outward toward multiple people or a group.
tips: Use a slight sweeping point for groups.
Keep the hand relaxed and natural.
Face the direction of the people clearly.
Make the movement easy to follow.

Their/Theirs.description: Move an open palm toward the referenced group.
tips: Keep the palm open and relaxed.
Direct the motion toward the group clearly.
Use a smooth outward movement.
Keep finger positioning neat and consistent.

Like.description: Place your right hand on your chest with your thumb and middle finger pinched together, then pull the hand away from the chest while extending the thumb and middle finger outward.
tips: Start with the middle finger and thumb lightly touching the chest.
The pull away is smooth, fingers extend open as the hand moves forward.
End with the thumb and middle finger clearly extended.

Cut.description: Extend your right index and middle fingers in a V-shape, then snap them shut like scissors in a cutting motion.
tips: Start with the V fingers fully open and extended.
The snap shut is quick and crisp, like a pair of scissors closing.
Emphasize the wrist-driven scissor snap.
The motion can be repeated twice.

Drop.description: Hold your right hand in front of you with fingers loosely spread and pointing forward, then let the wrist drop downward so the fingers fall toward the floor.
tips: Start with the hand raised and fingers loosely open.
The drop is a sudden downward wrist fall.
End with fingers hanging loosely downward.

Need.description: Extend your right index finger pointing forward, then bend it downward sharply from the wrist in a hooking motion.
tips: Start with the index finger fully extended.
The bend is a sharp downward hook, the wrist drives the motion.
The finger ends up bent or hooked downward.

Hear.description: Place your right hand near your ear with the thumb resting close to it, then curl your index and middle fingers in and out in a gentle repeated pulsing motion.
tips: The thumb stays anchored near the ear.
Only the index and middle fingers move.
The pulsing motion is soft and gentle.

Talk.description: Hold your right hand near your mouth with fingers together pointing upward, then open and close the fingers repeatedly like a mouth speaking.
tips: The fingers open and close at the knuckles.
Keep the hand close to your own mouth.
Emphasize the repeated open-close finger motion.
The repetition is key.

Eat.description: Bring the fingertips of your right hand together into a flat O shape and tap them toward your mouth repeatedly.
tips: Fingertips and thumb are all pinched together.
The tapping motion moves toward the mouth from the wrist.
Emphasize the repeated wrist-driven tapping motion.
Keep the motion small and natural.

Drink.description: Form a C-shape with your right hand as if holding a cup, then tilt the hand upward toward your mouth in a drinking motion.
tips: The C-shape should look like you are gripping an invisible cup.
The tilting motion comes from the wrist rotating upward.
End with the hand near the mouth and wrist fully tilted.

Help.description: Place your left hand flat with palm facing up. Rest your right hand in a thumbs-up shape on top of it, then lift both hands upward together.
tips: The flat base hand acts as a platform.
Both hands move together as one unit.
Keep the thumb clearly extended upward.
The upward movement should be smooth and deliberate.

See.description: Hold your right hand in a V-shape with the two fingers pointing toward your eyes, then rotate the wrist and point the V fingers outward away from your face.
tips: Start with the V fingers pointing back at your own eyes.
The wrist rotation forward is the key motion.
Keep both fingers of the V fully extended.
End with the V fingers pointing clearly forward.

Go.description: Extend both index fingers pointing forward, then arc them outward and away from your body simultaneously.
tips: Both hands start near the center and sweep outward together.
The outward arc comes from both wrists rotating away from the body.
Emphasize the wrist-driven outward motion.
End with both index fingers pointing clearly forward.

Come.description: Extend both index fingers pointing upward or outward, then arc them inward toward your body in a beckoning motion.
tips: Both index fingers start pointing away and curve inward.
The arcing motion comes from the wrists rotating inward.
Emphasize the wrist-driven inward arc.
The motion naturally reads as 'come here'.

Sit.description: Hold both hands in front of you with index and middle fingers extended together on each hand. Place the right hand fingers over the left fingers so they hang over like legs sitting on a bench.
tips: Both hands use the same two-finger shape.
The right hand fingers drape over the left ones.
Emphasize the downward wrist drape.
Hold the position briefly.

Stand.description: Hold your right hand flat with palm facing up. Place your right V-fingers pointing downward onto the base palm, standing upright like legs on a floor.
tips: The V fingers represent legs standing firmly.
Unlike Jump, the V fingers stay planted.
Emphasize a firm wrist placement.
Hold the position steadily.

Wake Up.description: Place both hands near the sides of your face with fingers pinched closed near your eyes, then flick them open as if eyes snapping open upon waking.
tips: Both hands start with fingertips pinched beside each eye.
The flick open is quick and simultaneous.
Pair with wide open eyes and a surprised expression.

Sleep.description: Press both flat hands together with fingers pointing upward, then tilt them sideways toward your cheek and rest your head against them like laying on a pillow.
tips: Both palms press together flat.
The head tilts to meet the hands.
Emphasize the wrist tilt sideways.

Stop.description: Hold your left hand flat with palm facing up. Bring your right flat hand down sharply onto the left palm in a chopping motion.
tips: The base hand stays completely still.
The chop is sharp and decisive.
Emphasize the wrist snapping downward.
The right hand lands perpendicular to the base hand.

Clean.description: Hold your left hand flat with palm facing up. Slide your right flat hand across it from wrist to fingertips in a smooth wiping motion.
tips: The left hand acts as the surface being cleaned.
The wipe travels the full length of the base palm.
The motion can be repeated twice.

Want.description: Hold both hands out in front of you with fingers spread and palms facing up, then pull them inward toward your body while curling the fingers into a claw shape.
tips: Start with both hands open and extended forward.
The pulling motion is inward toward the body.
Fingers curl into a claw as the hands pull in.
Both hands move symmetrically and simultaneously.

Cook.description: Hold your left hand flat with palm facing up. Place your right flat hand on top of it, then flip the right hand over so the back of the hand faces down.
tips: The left hand is the pan, keep it flat and still.
The right hand flips over smoothly.
The flipping motion can be repeated once.

Write.description: Hold your left hand flat with palm facing up. Pinch your right index finger and thumb together as if holding a pen and make small writing motions across the base palm.
tips: The pinched finger and thumb mimic holding a pen.
The writing motion is small and quick.
Emphasize the wrist-driven writing movement.
Unlike Draw which uses the pinky, Write uses the pinched fingertips.

Draw.description: Hold your left hand flat with palm facing up. Use your right pinky finger to trace a wavy or zigzag line across the base palm.
tips: The pinky acts like a pencil drawing on the palm.
The wavy motion comes from the wrist guiding the pinky.
Emphasize the wrist-driven drawing motion to distinguish it from Write.
Keep the other fingers of the right hand loosely curled.

Read.description: Hold your left hand flat with palm facing up to represent a page. Point your right V-fingers downward toward the base palm and move them back and forth across it like scanning text.
tips: The V fingers represent eyes scanning.
The back-and-forth motion mimics reading lines of text.
Emphasize the wrist-driven scanning motion.
The base hand stays flat and still.

Cry.description: Extend both index fingers and place them just below each eye, then draw them downward down the cheeks in a motion tracing falling tears.
tips: Both index fingers start just under the eyes.
The downward stroke is slow and deliberate.
Emphasize the wrist-guided downward draw.
The motion can be repeated.

Laugh.description: Point both index fingers toward the corners of your mouth, then pull them outward and upward in a motion that traces a smile.
tips: Both index fingers start at the corners of the mouth.
The outward and upward pull mimics the shape of a smile.
Emphasize the wrist-driven outward flick.
Pair with a genuine smile expression.

Walk.description: Hold both hands flat in front of you with palms facing down, then alternate moving them forward one at a time in a stepping motion.
tips: Both hands represent feet, they alternate.
Each hand moves forward from the wrist.
Emphasize the alternating wrist-forward motion.
Keep a steady rhythm.

Jump.description: Hold your left hand flat with palm facing up. Place your right hand in a V-shape with fingers pointing down onto the base palm, then spring the V fingers upward off the palm.
tips: The V fingers represent legs, they start bent then spring up.
The launching motion comes from the wrist flicking upward.
Emphasize the wrist spring upward.
The base hand stays completely still.

Hug.description: Cross both arms over your chest with hands in fists, as if wrapping your arms around someone, then squeeze the arms slightly inward toward the chest.
tips: Both arms cross at the wrists with fists resting on opposite shoulders.
The squeeze inward is a subtle but deliberate motion.
Keep both fists firm throughout the motion.

Where.description: Extend your right index finger pointing upward and wave it side to side from the wrist.
tips: Only the index finger is extended, keep all other fingers curled in.
The waving motion comes from the wrist, not the whole arm.
Keep the hand raised at about chin to shoulder level.
Furrow your brows to signal the WH question expression.

Who.description: Make an L-shape with your right hand, then place the tip of your index finger on your chin and rotate or wiggle it slightly.
tips: The index finger rests lightly on the chin.
The small circular or wiggling motion is what distinguishes it.
Keep the rest of your fingers relaxed and curled.
Pair with a furrowed brow to complete the question expression.

Why.description: Touch your right middle finger to your forehead, then pull the hand away while transitioning into a loose bent-finger handshape in front of you.
tips: The middle finger makes brief contact with the forehead before pulling away.
As the hand moves forward, let the fingers naturally curve into a loose claw shape.
The movement represents a thought leaving your head.
Furrow your brows to complete the WH question facial expression.

How.description: Place both hands together in front of you with knuckles touching and thumbs pointing up. Roll both hands forward and upward, ending with palms facing up.
tips: Start with the knuckles of both hands touching each other.
The rolling motion is smooth, think of opening a book forward.
End clearly with palms facing upward and fingers relaxed.
Furrow your brows to complete the WH question facial expression.

What.description: Hold both hands out in front of you with palms facing upward and fingers loosely spread, then tilt them slightly side to side as if presenting nothing.
tips: Both hands move together in a loose, relaxed shrug-like motion.
Keep fingers naturally spread, not stiff or tightly together.
The motion is subtle, not exaggerated, a gentle side-to-side tilt.
Furrow your brows and pair with a slight shoulder shrug for full expression.

When.description: Hold your left index finger pointing upward. With your right index finger, draw a circle around it, then tap the tip of the left finger.
tips: The left finger stays completely still as the base.
The right finger circles smoothly before landing on the fingertip.
Both fingers should be close together, the circle is small and tight.
Furrow your brows to complete the WH question facial expression.

Which.description: Hold both hands in front of you with thumbs pointing up. Alternate moving them up and down like a scale weighing two options.
tips: Both hands start at the same height before the alternating motion begins.
The up-down alternating movement is smooth and even, not jerky.
Keep the thumbs clearly extended upward throughout the motion.
Furrow your brows to complete the WH question facial expression.

Later.description: Form an L-shape with your right hand (thumb up, index finger pointing up). Then rotate the hand forward and downward so the index finger ends up pointing downward or away from you.
tips: Keep the L-shape firm throughout the entire movement.
The bigger the tilt forward, the further into the future 'later' implies.
Practice the smooth wrist pivot from vertical to horizontal or downward.

Again.description: Hold your left hand flat with palm facing up. Form a curved hand with your dominant hand, then arc it down so the fingertips land on the center of the flat palm.
tips: Keep the base hand still and relaxed with palm fully facing up.
The moving hand should arc smoothly, like tapping the palm with a gentle curved scoop.
Fingertips should land near the center of the base palm, not the edge.
The motion can be repeated twice to emphasize the meaning.

Done.description: Hold both hands in front of you with fingers bent and palms facing inward. Flip both hands outward simultaneously so the palms end up facing away from you with fingers spread open.
tips: The flip should be quick and definitive, it signals a clear end.
Both hands move at the same time and mirror each other.
Start with fingers loosely curled inward before the flip.
End with fingers fully spread and palms clearly facing outward.

More.description: Form flat O shapes with both hands by bringing all fingertips to meet the thumb on each hand. Tap the fingertips of both hands together in front of your body.
tips: Both hands should mirror each other with fingertips pinched together.
The tapping motion can be done twice to make the sign clear.
Keep the hands at a comfortable mid-chest height.
Make sure the fingertips, not the knuckles, are what make contact.

Now.description: Start with both index fingers pointing upward near your shoulders. Then drop both hands down simultaneously, ending with Y-handshapes (thumb and pinky extended) held out in front of you.
tips: The drop should be smooth and land at about waist or lower-chest level.
End clearly in the Y-handshape, thumb and pinky out, other fingers folded in.
Both hands move together at the same time.
The downward motion represents the present moment happening right now.

tipText: Hold still the sign for half a second until the translation appears. For motion signs, complete the full gesture naturally and wait for the translation to show before continuing. Visit the learning section to gain more knowledge about ASL.
